plays.title: Author the RFP before competitors enter
plays.guidance: When the buyer is about to launch a tender, hand the champion your own RFP question template and run pre-RFP 'to-be' process workshops so the eventual RFP scores your differentiators. Get this done before any competitor is engaged so rivals respond to criteria framed around your strengths.

proof.fact: Gave champion Marina an RFP question template on first demo; deal qualified same week and rivals responded to a Zycus-framed tender
Buyer authored RFP off Zycus template after pre-RFP workshops, putting Coupa and Ariba on the back foot
Zycus helped create the RFP; Coupa and Ariba responded to requirements written around Zycus capability

plays.title: Reframe a tool swap as a transformation
plays.guidance: Run deep discovery to surface the buyer's real operating pain (lean teams, manual offline work, board-level themes) and reframe the conversation from feature comparison to a multi-year transformation narrative tied to their corporate strategy. This earns the right to a strategic, multi-product story instead of a price fight, and makes the internal owner the seller.

proof.fact: Discovery surfaced JAGGAER was used only as a passive repository and AI/transformation was a board theme; reframed from tool-swap to transformation
Mapped VP Reengineering's stated modules to an end-to-end S2P transformation story so she became the internal seller
Tied the suite to named corporate strategy (improve margins, cash conversion, deleverage) for CEO sign-off

plays.title: Anchor proof on the buyer's named pain with a real-user POC
plays.guidance: Build the demo around the buyer's explicitly stated gaps (usability, integration, adoption fear) rather than a feature tour, then stand up a free, time-boxed, hands-on POC on their own data and users with success criteria agreed up front. Let risk-averse buyers de-risk the decision themselves and convert abstract claims into observed, defensible proof.

proof.fact: Free 2.5-week POC with ~30 real users in a live sandbox; answered Coupa SpendGuard on the buyer's own turf
Binary go/no-go pilot (adoption = yes); 7 of 7 pilot respondents unanimously chose to keep e-signature
Live Drive Test on real MRO data, anchored on the buyer's prior failed eProc project, beat a rival head-to-head on usability

plays.title: Prove value inside the tool they already use
plays.guidance: Embed the pilot or demo directly in the buyer's existing system (Teams/Slack intake, EHR chart, ERP) so users experience the product without leaving their workflow. In-flow proof removes the change-management objection and makes adoption feel low-effort.

proof.fact: Certinal e-signature embedded in Epic EHR chart review; clinicians signed without leaving workflow
Microsoft Teams-native intake via the firm's own supplier form was cited as the adoption edge over the remaining finalist

plays.title: Supply industry-twin references as an award gate
plays.guidance: Line up reference customers that match the buyer's vertical, scale, and architecture (same industry, same incumbent, same integration), plus analyst proof, timed to the shortlist or award gate. Peer proof answers 'has anyone like us done this' far more credibly than vendor claims and can be made an explicit award criterion.

proof.fact: Shared Hapag-Lloyd marine case study and three reference letters to a maritime buyer
Benchmark Senior Living reference chosen specifically because they run Zycus integrated with Yardi
Made reference calls a requirement to award; two references attended Horizon to validate live

plays.title: Lead with working, embedded AI as the tie-breaker
plays.guidance: Position productised AI (Merlin intake in Teams/Slack, GenAI extraction, Negogenie redlining, autonomous negotiation) as a live differentiator tied to the buyer's actual processes, sequenced after core fit is satisfied so AI is the tie-breaker. Absorb token costs to remove the adoption objection.

proof.fact: GenAI on GPT-4o and Claude, Negogenie redlining, AP SmartDesk; Zycus absorbed LLM token costs
AI/Merlin sessions sequenced after Finance was satisfied became the reason to pick Zycus over Coupa
Led with Merlin Teams intake and an autonomous negotiation agent against incumbent SAP Ariba

plays.title: Expose the incumbent as repository-grade / never-adopted
plays.guidance: Surface evidence that the incumbent is delivering only partial or repository-grade value and was never fully adopted, so the switching-cost objection collapses before it is raised. Frame the purchase as recovering unrealised value rather than a like-for-like swap.

proof.fact: Showed JAGGAER delivered only repository value despite being incumbent S2P
Anchored the case on Ivalua signed 2018 but never fully live; positioned as recovering unrealised value

plays.title: Scope a modular foot-in-the-door the incumbent cannot match
plays.guidance: When the incumbent refuses to unbundle or the buyer cannot afford a full suite, deliberately scope a standalone, affordable, low-risk entry module with the larger displacement as the follow-on. Match the buyer's decentralised or cost-pressured reality with something the incumbent structurally will not sell.

proof.fact: Scoped standalone eProc at ~200k after JAGGAER refused to unbundle; S2P displacement as follow-on
De-scoped to a right-sized standalone S2C for a near-greenfield, cash-constrained gov buyer

plays.title: Coexist with the incumbent instead of fighting it
plays.guidance: When the buyer has a sunk ERP/P2P investment and no appetite to rip-and-replace, position as an integration layer that complements the incumbent (win S2C, leave their P2P) with consistent data flow. This removes the switching-cost and political risk that favours the entrenched vendor.

proof.fact: Framed as complement-not-replace to SAP Ariba, removing the 'we already have Ariba' blocker
Won S2C while Ariba stayed for P2P; validated with a Dow reference running the same hybrid

plays.title: Displace DocuSign with native, embedded e-signature
plays.guidance: Position Certinal as a drop-in DocuSign replacement scoped one-to-one to the buyer's exact signing workflow, or embed it inside CLM/iContract so the standalone signing tool becomes redundant. Win on switching economics, consolidation, and capability (envelope size, NPS, compliance), not just features.

proof.fact: Replaced DocuSign on cost and switching economics with a first-year price concession to avoid double-pay
Drop-in Certinal replacement after the buyer discontinued DocuSign, matching the existing workflow one-to-one
Embedded Certinal e-sign inside iContract CLM so standalone DocuSign became redundant

plays.title: Arm the champion with a head-to-head comparison
plays.guidance: Hand the buyer a tailored Zycus-vs-competitor comparison deck (and FUD slides where price-led rivals attack) right before the shortlist or award, so the internal advocate carries your differentiation narrative into rooms you are not in. Re-anchor the decision on capability and TCO when a competitor competes on price.

proof.fact: Shared a Zycus-vs-Coupa comparison deck with the revised BAFO before the award
Sent Coupa FUD slides and went 'on Coupa attack vs AI' to neutralise aggressive Coupa pricing
Countered Coupa Gartner-position FUD with a stronger TCO model and Forrester position as committee ammo

plays.title: Turn a product gap into a co-designed roadmap commitment
plays.guidance: When a real gap surfaces (UX, a discontinued feature, a missing comparison capability), do not deflect: run a live wireframe/scoping session, co-design the enhancement with the champion, and make a dated contractual commitment. Turning a weakness into a partnership removes the main reason to pick a specialist competitor.

proof.fact: Ran a live wireframe session on Negogenie inline editing, neutralising CLM specialist Sirion Labs
Committed contractually to deliver catalog-vs-PO comparison the buyer had elevated to a decision criterion

plays.title: Co-build the ROI business case with finance
plays.guidance: When budget is the named blocker, build the savings/TCO model jointly with the buyer's finance lead off their own spend data, with realistic high-confidence targets, so the numbers become the buyer's own artefact. A co-owned business case converts a budget objection into a funded, CFO-defensible project.

proof.fact: Met the CFO and ran a value-engineering/ROI workstream to turn budget from a blocker into a funded project
Co-built a $59M addressable-spend model with the finance lead so the case was the buyer's own
Value-engineering/TCO workshop gave champions the ROI artefact to win CEO budget approval

plays.title: Anchor early to the budget-holding exec
plays.guidance: Engage the economic buyer (CPO/CFO/SVP who signs and owns the pain) from qualification, confirm budget authority and any HQ mandate up front, and tie the pitch to their stated corporate financial strategy. The deal never stalls waiting for budget authority or sign-off.

proof.fact: Anchored to Stuart Holmes (SVP Finance + CFO of US entity) with confirmed HQ permission to proceed
Anchored on a CEO-reporting CPO who personally drove scheduling, data, and signature
Brought a VP value-engineering sponsor into the early business case alongside the champion hired to build it

plays.title: Run exec-to-exec air cover to unblock a stall
plays.guidance: When a non-champion evaluator or procurement silence stalls a deal, escalate VP/CRO-to-economic-buyer to unblock the commercial track, and use a transparency-forcing ask ('tell us if we are out') to force a commit-or-disqualify. Senior in-region presence at the close signals commitment and clears legal/signatory friction.

proof.fact: VP ran a 1:1 with the economic buyer to unblock the minimum-commitment blocker when the SPOC was not a champion
Escalated through the CIO to the Head of Procurement with a transparency ask to re-energise a stalled deal
Connected CRO Denise Miller to the CFO to align contract finalisation against the DOJ deadline

plays.title: Multi-thread across every veto-holding function
plays.guidance: Map and engage the full buying committee (procurement, finance, IT/integration, legal, compliance/privacy, security, tax, ESG) with role-specific sessions so no single gatekeeper can stall or veto. Run dedicated tracks per function so each board can be satisfied in parallel.

proof.fact: Engaged 50+ contacts across procurement, IT/SAP, legal, compliance, security, treasury and tax with dedicated tracks
Widened to a 20-contact group with a separate Mexico compliance call and AP-focused CVT call
Worked a 23-contact committee across Finance, IT and Legal so no Coupa-friendly stakeholder could steer it

plays.title: Build a dual champion and enable them to self-validate
plays.guidance: Pair the IT/technical buyer with the procurement/process owner so the business case carries both technical credibility and procurement ownership, and let the champion reach their own verdict through neutral third parties (Gartner/Forrester, peer references). Arm them with the materials to sell upward themselves; a self-validated champion sells internally far harder than any vendor.

proof.fact: Armed a dual champion (IT Category Manager + Director of Procurement) who secured board approval to replace iValua
Champion reached his own Gartner 'gold standard' verdict, then co-built a mutual close plan
Enabled CPO Kristi Allen with the full-suite business case to present to CFO and CEO and beat the internal Medius push

plays.title: Stand up a customer-side adoption / quarterback engine
plays.guidance: Where adoption is fragile or the org is decentralised, enable a credible internal owner to run awareness emails, tip sheets, drop-in sessions, scheduling, and pre-work routing, while you supply the artefacts. An internal owner driving enablement turns a shaky early-adoption curve into a decisive keep decision and keeps a multi-department buyer moving.

proof.fact: Program owner John Tuman ran provider emails, tip sheets and drop-ins; usage recovered to a unanimous keep vote
Converted a procurement contact into project lead Ben Cutting who quarterbacked scheduling and pre-work across departments

plays.title: Compress the funnel and anchor to a compelling event
plays.guidance: Drive fast, decisive stage advances while pain is fresh to deny incumbents time to re-entrench, and anchor late-stage cadence to a real external compelling event (incumbent expiry, fiscal year-end, regulatory deadline) to force a go/no-go and compress legal/security review. A real deadline removes 'no decision' as an option.

proof.fact: Used JAGGAER June 28 expiry to force a mid-March go/no-go and a 90-day MVP go-live
Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted in 20 days, locking Zycus in before alternatives could be sourced
Used 30-Sep fiscal year-end as a forcing function with pricing valid only on a pre-deadline signature

plays.title: Land a small paid pilot with an expansion trigger
plays.guidance: For low-maturity, slow-procurement, or pre-mandated buyers, land a small fixed-scope paid pilot that fits inside fast approval authority, with a quantified upsell trigger written into the order form; or for genuinely simple use cases run one full-journey demo straight to a lightweight close path. The buyer commits readily when the larger spend is conditional on demonstrated results.

proof.fact: Pilot order form (1000 transactions, 3 months) with a 10x expansion trigger; Qualified to Won in 9 days
Landed a 1,000 USD Certinal pilot to keep the decision inside IT/management authority
Qualified as a satellite adopter of a URW mandate; jumped Initial Interest to Closed Won in one motion

plays.title: Run the deal through a trusted local or advisory partner
plays.guidance: In regions where geography and implementation risk are objections, run the evaluation, BAFO submissions, and board timeline through a trusted local implementation partner; or use a trusted advisory partner (KPMG) for a warm C-suite intro before the RFP exists. Partner coverage neutralises the offshore-vendor objection and gets you shaping scope early.

proof.fact: Letsema carried BAFO submissions and managed the board timeline on the ground in South Africa
Used KPMG to get a warm intro to the Group CFO and Supply Chain VP before the RFP existed

plays.title: De-risk adoption with a named change-management partner
plays.guidance: When the buyer names adoption or change management as the #1 risk, bring in a credible third-party change-management/implementation partner scoped to a tightly-defined engagement priced near the buyer's cost ceiling. Pairing a partner with a phased MVP rollout de-risks the internal sell and makes the number defensible.

proof.fact: Brought in Hackett Group as change-management partner priced to keep total cost near the ~300k ceiling
Bundled Velocity Procurement (FS/insurance-experienced) while committing Zycus owned ~80% of integration
Re-scoped to on-site 'boots on the ground' change management within 48 hours during BAFO

plays.title: Be the software inside multiple SI bids and cut their TCO
plays.guidance: Where the buyer mandates purchase through a System Integrator, position your software inside two or more competing SI bids and fine-tune commercials for each so you win regardless of which SI is selected, and actively coach the SI prime to cut its implementation quote since services cost (not software) blows the CFO budget. The vendor, not the buyer, should own the TCO narrative.

proof.fact: Positioned Zycus inside two of three SI bids (TCS and Impressive) and coached the SIs on differentiators
Coached TCS to submit under $3m implementation and ran a joint TCS+Zycus exec session with the CFO
Offered Zycus-led delivery to remove ~$900k SI TCO risk threatening the business case

plays.title: Solve the buyer's real economic problem, do not just discount
plays.guidance: Trade non-cost flexibility (deferred deposits, milestone payments, free Year 1 in lieu of services, currency conversion, term length, configuration headroom, reference rights) for the things you value, so the price feels fair and future-proof. Deferred-deposit terms can specifically neutralise the cost of running two vendors in parallel during cutover.

proof.fact: 20% deposit deferred 90 days to absorb JAGGAER overlap, plus phased scope, in exchange for reference rights
Offered free Year 1 in lieu of Zycus professional services rather than discounting the license
Held first-year price on a 6-year volume rationale, cut bundle prices, and re-denominated USD to EUR

plays.title: Right-size licensing and pricing to actual usage
plays.guidance: Re-quote around the buyer's true consumption profile (internal vs external transactions, power users vs low-frequency requestors, real invoice/contract volumes and page lengths) instead of a flat envelope. A defensible, consumption-based number removes the commercial objection without a race to the bottom, even when you are the priciest finalist.

proof.fact: Re-quoted Certinal on the buyer's internal-vs-external transaction ratio
Reframed 1000 'users' as mostly low-frequency requestors to size eProc realistically and stay in scope as the priciest finalist
Rebalanced to 45,000 real invoices and raised per-page thresholds to fit a law firm's long documents

plays.title: Upsell term and footprint at the moment of selection
plays.guidance: When the buyer has just chosen you for a multi-year strategic platform, raise the contract term (3 to 5 years) and expand committed users or countries while they are leaning in, growing TCV with no resistance. Trade concessions the buyer values (no tier penalties, dropped page count) for longer term and more committed users.

proof.fact: Upsold the term from 3 to 5 years at the moment of selection, confirmed before MSA drafting
Anchored 4+2, landed a 5-year deal, grew committed users off punitive tiering and dropped page metering
Standardised per-country expansion pricing to grow footprint across 8+2 operating countries

plays.title: Remove invoice/PO friction and put a cost on slippage
plays.guidance: Take the last objections off the table by aligning billing to actual go-live, invoicing on emailed finance confirmation without a PO, and dropping unneeded modules; and tie a dated commercial impact (a $0 TTVR, or a time-boxed no-regret best-and-final) to any delay. This clears the 'paying before we use it' and procurement-lead-time blockers and converts the buyer's internal delays into a shared deadline.

proof.fact: Dropped the unneeded compliance module and set start date to Feb 1 to align billing with go-live
Allowed invoicing without a PO on emailed finance confirmation to hold the kickoff date
Prepared a $0 TTVR documenting commercial impact for delays beyond the June 1 go-live

plays.title: Defend the commercial floor with a supporting business case
plays.guidance: Hold firm on the commercial floor (minimum user commitment, no itemised unbundling without higher commercials, no rate-card discount) while conceding on low-stakes terms, and bring in a business-case/cost-estimate to give procurement internal cover. Make the buyer reveal their target number first, and pause negotiation to reset unreasonable demands.

proof.fact: Held a 500-user minimum from day 1 against a flexibility-seeking French procurement team, backed by a business case
Refused to itemise bundled pricing without higher commercials and made the buyer submit their numbers first
Walked back from the table once to force common sense, then closed an accepted 3-year term via repeated BAFOs

plays.title: Clear data-sovereignty, sanctions and security gates fast
plays.guidance: Treat regulatory gates (GDPR/PDPA/PDP transfer assessments, sanctions controls, SOC 2/VAPT findings, GenAI governance) as the real deal-blocker: walk legal/DPO/InfoSec through your transfer-impact assessment, certifications, sub-processor designations, and a remediation evidence package, supplying documentation within 24 hours. A precise, fast written answer turns a disqualifier into a trust signal and can neutralise a rival's only security advantage.

proof.fact: Cleared the Malaysian PDPA transfer-assessment blocker with certs and a Telekom Malaysia precedent within 24 hours
Answered GDPR annexes and secured a DPA deviation for EU-to-India transfer plus QTSP coverage
Reframed all 42 VAPT findings as low-risk with a SOC 2 bridge letter, neutralising the rival's one InfoSec edge

plays.title: Lead with governance and master the mandated gov process
plays.guidance: For monitored or gov-linked buyers, reframe the purchase from efficiency to a governance/compliance mandate (segregation of duties, fraud prevention, 3-bid-and-buy), and show fluency in the mandated procurement process (Competitive Dialogue, JSD/BAFO, QITC framework, vendor-registration scorecards, routing comms through the neutral advisor). Compliance is the board-level reason to buy, and respecting the process builds procurement trust that clears the gate.

proof.fact: Reframed the S2P RFP as a DOJ-monitorship governance mandate, undercutting transaction-led rivals
Engaged the Competitive Dialogue on its own terms and adopted the QITC two-phase contracting framework
Supported the champion's two-board-paper self-funding strategy and whole-of-government lighthouse vision

plays.title: Run legal/commercial tracks in parallel and bridge the SOW
plays.guidance: Share MSA, Order and SOW together for concurrent legal review, run scope workshops alongside redlining, own version control and paper turnaround, and reuse previously approved redlines. When signature is gated on a full SOW that needs exact counts, issue an assumptions-based or bridge SOW so the order form and MSA sign on time while the full SOW finishes later.

proof.fact: Shared MSA, Order and SOW together for concurrent legal review and ran scoping in parallel
Structured a Bridge SOW alongside the Order Form and MSA so signature was not gated on the full SOW
Reused 2023 approved redlines and escalated the non-negotiable bank-security clause senior-to-senior

plays.title: Own integration, migration and region-specific compliance
plays.guidance: Address the buyer's biggest execution fear by leading with pre-built ERP adapters/iSaaS, owning data migration QC, writing integration-ownership into explicit SOW language, and solving the hardest regional compliance objection (Mexico CFDI, China rules, local QES) out-of-the-box via pre-integrated partners with no additional integration effort. Removing a cost line competitors scope as custom work is a regional differentiator.

proof.fact: Led with ready-to-use SAP/Oracle adapters and iSaaS to make the multi-ERP estate a solved problem vs iValua/Ariba/Coupa
Showed Mexican CFDI validation via pre-integrated Pegaso to SAP with zero additional integration effort
Attached iSaaS Advanced so Zycus owns ongoing Yardi integration upkeep, a turnkey commitment
